| MCP:xGuxDl | # vi: ft=dosini
| MCP:xGuxDl | [main]
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Enables context sensitive auto-completion. If this is disabled, all
| MCP:xGuxDl | # possible completions will be listed.
| MCP:xGuxDl | smart_completion = True
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Enable auto suggestions (like Fish shell).
| MCP:xGuxDl | auto_suggest = True
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Display the completions in several columns. (More completions will be
| MCP:xGuxDl | # visible.)
| MCP:xGuxDl | wider_completion_menu = False
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Do not create new connections for refreshing completions; Equivalent to
| MCP:xGuxDl | # always running with the --single-connection flag.
| MCP:xGuxDl | always_use_single_connection = False
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Multi-line mode allows breaking up the sql statements into multiple lines. If
| MCP:xGuxDl | # this is set to True, then the end of the statements must have a semi-colon.
| MCP:xGuxDl | # If this is set to False then sql statements can't be split into multiple
| MCP:xGuxDl | # lines. End of line (return) is considered as the end of the statement.
| MCP:xGuxDl | multi_line = False
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # If multi_line_mode is set to "psql", in multi-line mode, [Enter] will execute
| MCP:xGuxDl | # the current input if the input ends in a semicolon.
| MCP:xGuxDl | # If multi_line_mode is set to "safe", in multi-line mode, [Enter] will always
| MCP:xGuxDl | # insert a newline, and [Esc] [Enter] or [Alt]-[Enter] must be used to execute
| MCP:xGuxDl | # a command.
| MCP:xGuxDl | multi_line_mode = psql
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Destructive warning will alert you before executing a sql statement
| MCP:xGuxDl | # that may cause harm to the database such as "drop table", "drop database",
| MCP:xGuxDl | # "shutdown", "delete", or "update".
| MCP:xGuxDl | # You can pass a list of destructive commands or leave it empty if you want to skip all warnings.
| MCP:xGuxDl | # "unconditional_update" will warn you of update statements that don't have a where clause
| MCP:xGuxDl | destructive_warning = drop, shutdown, delete, truncate, alter, update, unconditional_update
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # When `destructive_warning` is on and the user declines to proceed with a
| MCP:xGuxDl | # destructive statement, the current transaction (if any) is left untouched,
| MCP:xGuxDl | # by default. When setting `destructive_warning_restarts_connection` to
| MCP:xGuxDl | # "True", the connection to the server is restarted. In that case, the
| MCP:xGuxDl | # transaction (if any) is rolled back.
| MCP:xGuxDl | destructive_warning_restarts_connection = False
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # When this option is on (and if `destructive_warning` is not empty),
| MCP:xGuxDl | # destructive statements are not executed when outside of a transaction.
| MCP:xGuxDl | destructive_statements_require_transaction = False
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Enables expand mode, which is similar to `\x` in psql.
| MCP:xGuxDl | expand = False
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Enables auto expand mode, which is similar to `\x auto` in psql.
| MCP:xGuxDl | auto_expand = False
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Auto-retry queries on connection failures and other operational errors. If
| MCP:xGuxDl | # False, will prompt to rerun the failed query instead of auto-retrying.
| MCP:xGuxDl | auto_retry_closed_connection = True
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # If set to True, table suggestions will include a table alias
| MCP:xGuxDl | generate_aliases = False
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Path to a json file that specifies specific table aliases to use when generate_aliases is set to True
| MCP:xGuxDl | # the format for this file should be:
| MCP:xGuxDl | # {
| MCP:xGuxDl | #     "some_table_name": "desired_alias",
| MCP:xGuxDl | #     "some_other_table_name": "another_alias"
| MCP:xGuxDl | # }
| MCP:xGuxDl | alias_map_file =
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # log_file location.
| MCP:xGuxDl | # In Unix/Linux: ~/.config/pgcli/log
| MCP:xGuxDl | # In Windows: %USERPROFILE%\AppData\Local\dbcli\pgcli\log
| MCP:xGuxDl | # %USERPROFILE% is typically C:\Users\{username}
| MCP:xGuxDl | log_file = default
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # keyword casing preference. Possible values: "lower", "upper", "auto"
| MCP:xGuxDl | keyword_casing = auto
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # casing_file location.
| MCP:xGuxDl | # In Unix/Linux: ~/.config/pgcli/casing
| MCP:xGuxDl | # In Windows: %USERPROFILE%\AppData\Local\dbcli\pgcli\casing
| MCP:xGuxDl | # %USERPROFILE% is typically C:\Users\{username}
| MCP:xGuxDl | casing_file = default
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # If generate_casing_file is set to True and there is no file in the above
| MCP:xGuxDl | # location, one will be generated based on usage in SQL/PLPGSQL functions.
| MCP:xGuxDl | generate_casing_file = False
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Casing of column headers based on the casing_file described above
| MCP:xGuxDl | case_column_headers = True
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # history_file location.
| MCP:xGuxDl | # In Unix/Linux: ~/.config/pgcli/history
| MCP:xGuxDl | # In Windows: %USERPROFILE%\AppData\Local\dbcli\pgcli\history
| MCP:xGuxDl | # %USERPROFILE% is typically C:\Users\{username}
| MCP:xGuxDl | history_file = default
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Default log level. Possible values: "CRITICAL", "ERROR", "WARNING", "INFO"
| MCP:xGuxDl | # and "DEBUG". "NONE" disables logging.
| MCP:xGuxDl | log_level = INFO
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Order of columns when expanding * to column list
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Possible values: "table_order" and "alphabetic"
| MCP:xGuxDl | asterisk_column_order = table_order
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Whether to qualify with table alias/name when suggesting columns
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Possible values: "always", "never" and "if_more_than_one_table"
| MCP:xGuxDl | qualify_columns = if_more_than_one_table
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # When no schema is entered, only suggest objects in search_path
| MCP:xGuxDl | search_path_filter = False
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Default pager. See https://www.pgcli.com/pager for more information on settings.
| MCP:xGuxDl | # By default 'PAGER' environment variable is used. If the pager is less, and the 'LESS'
| MCP:xGuxDl | # environment variable is not set, then LESS='-SRXF' will be automatically set.
| MCP:xGuxDl | # pager = less
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Timing of sql statements and table rendering.
| MCP:xGuxDl | timing = True
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Hide the query text when executing named queries (\n <name>).
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Only the query results will be displayed.
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Can be toggled at runtime with \nq command.
| MCP:xGuxDl | hide_named_query_text = False
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Show/hide the informational toolbar with function keymap at the footer.
| MCP:xGuxDl | show_bottom_toolbar = True
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Table format. Possible values: psql, plain, simple, grid, fancy_grid, pipe,
| MCP:xGuxDl | # ascii, double, github, orgtbl, rst, mediawiki, html, latex, latex_booktabs,
| MCP:xGuxDl | # textile, moinmoin, jira, vertical, tsv, csv, sql-insert, sql-update,
| MCP:xGuxDl | # sql-update-1, sql-update-2 (formatter with sql-* prefix can format query
| MCP:xGuxDl | # output to executable insertion or updating sql).
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Recommended: psql, fancy_grid and grid.
| MCP:xGuxDl | table_format = psql
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Syntax Style. Possible values: manni, igor, xcode, vim, autumn, vs, rrt,
| MCP:xGuxDl | # native, perldoc, borland, tango, emacs, friendly, monokai, paraiso-dark,
| MCP:xGuxDl | # colorful, murphy, bw, pastie, paraiso-light, trac, default, fruity
| MCP:xGuxDl | syntax_style = default
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Keybindings:
| MCP:xGuxDl | # When Vi mode is enabled you can use modal editing features offered by Vi in the REPL.
| MCP:xGuxDl | # When Vi mode is disabled emacs keybindings such as Ctrl-A for home and Ctrl-E
| MCP:xGuxDl | # for end are available in the REPL.
| MCP:xGuxDl | vi = False
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Error handling
| MCP:xGuxDl | # When one of multiple SQL statements causes an error, choose to either
| MCP:xGuxDl | # continue executing the remaining statements, or stopping
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Possible values "STOP" or "RESUME"
| MCP:xGuxDl | on_error = STOP
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Set threshold for row limit. Use 0 to disable limiting.
| MCP:xGuxDl | row_limit = 1000
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Truncate long text fields to this value for tabular display (does not apply to csv).
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Leave unset to disable truncation. Example: "max_field_width = "
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Be aware that formatting might get slow with values larger than 500 and tables with
| MCP:xGuxDl | # lots of records.
| MCP:xGuxDl | max_field_width = 500
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Skip intro on startup and goodbye on exit
| MCP:xGuxDl | less_chatty = False
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Show all Postgres error fields (as listed in
| MCP:xGuxDl | # https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/protocol-error-fields.html).
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Can be toggled with \v.
| MCP:xGuxDl | verbose_errors = False
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Postgres prompt
| MCP:xGuxDl | # \t - Current date and time
| MCP:xGuxDl | # \u - Username
| MCP:xGuxDl | # \h - Short hostname of the server (up to first '.')
| MCP:xGuxDl | # \H - Hostname of the server
| MCP:xGuxDl | # \d - Database name
| MCP:xGuxDl | # \p - Database port
| MCP:xGuxDl | # \i - Postgres PID
| MCP:xGuxDl | # \# - "@" sign if logged in as superuser, '>' in other case
| MCP:xGuxDl | # \n - Newline
| MCP:xGuxDl | # \T - Transaction status: '*' if in a valid transaction, '!' if in a failed transaction, '?' if disconnected, empty otherwise
| MCP:xGuxDl | # \dsn_alias - name of dsn connection string alias if -D option is used (empty otherwise)
| MCP:xGuxDl | # \x1b[...m - insert ANSI escape sequence
| MCP:xGuxDl | # eg: prompt = '\x1b[35m\u@\x1b[32m\h:\x1b[36m\d>'
| MCP:xGuxDl | prompt = '\u@\h:\d> '
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Number of lines to reserve for the suggestion menu

| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Character used to left pad multi-line queries to match the prompt size.

| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # The string used in place of a null value.

| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # manage pager on startup

| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Use keyring to automatically save and load password in a secure manner

| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Automatically set the session time zone to the local time zone

| MCP:xGuxDl | use_local_timezone = True
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Custom colors for the completion menu, toolbar, etc.
| MCP:xGuxDl | [colors]

| MCP:xGuxDl | scrollbar = 'bg:#00aaaa'

| MCP:xGuxDl | search.current = '#ffffff bg:#44aa44'

| MCP:xGuxDl | bottom-toolbar.on = 'bg:#222222 #ffffff'

| MCP:xGuxDl | # These three values can be used to further refine the syntax highlighting.

| MCP:xGuxDl | output.even-row = ""
| MCP:xGuxDl | output.null = "#808080"
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Named queries are queries you can execute by name.
| MCP:xGuxDl | [named queries]
| MCP:xGuxDl | # ver = "SELECT version()"
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Here's where you can provide a list of connection string aliases.
| MCP:xGuxDl | # You can use it by passing the -D option. `pgcli -D example_dsn`
| MCP:xGuxDl | [alias_dsn]

| MCP:xGuxDl | # Initial commands to execute when connecting to any database.
| MCP:xGuxDl | [init-commands]
| MCP:xGuxDl | # example = "SET search_path TO myschema"
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Initial commands to execute when connecting to a DSN alias.
| MCP:xGuxDl | [alias_dsn.init-commands]
| MCP:xGuxDl | # example_dsn = "SET search_path TO otherschema; SET timezone TO 'UTC'"
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Format for number representation
| MCP:xGuxDl | # for decimal "d" - 12345678, ",d" - 12,345,678
| MCP:xGuxDl | # for float "g" - 123456.78, ",g" - 123,456.78
| MCP:xGuxDl | [data_formats]
| MCP:xGuxDl | decimal = ""
| MCP:xGuxDl | float = ""
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Per column formats for date/timestamp columns
| MCP:xGuxDl | [column_date_formats]
| MCP:xGuxDl | # use strftime format, e.g.
| MCP:xGuxDl | # created = "%Y-%m-%d"
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Per host ssh tunnel configuration
| MCP:xGuxDl | [ssh tunnels]
| MCP:xGuxDl | # ^example.*\.host$ = myuser:mypasswd@my.tunnel.com:4000
| MCP:xGuxDl | # .*\.net = another.tunnel.com
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Per dsn_alias ssh tunnel configuration
| MCP:xGuxDl | [dsn ssh tunnels]
| MCP:xGuxDl | # ^example_dsn$  = myuser:mypasswd@my.tunnel.com:4000
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Audit logging
| MCP:xGuxDl | #
| MCP:xGuxDl | # Section consumed by the maintained pgcli packaging; an environment
| MCP:xGuxDl | # without the audit subsystem (e.g., a pip-installed checkout) treats
| MCP:xGuxDl | # these keys as no-ops, which is why they aren't documented in
| MCP:xGuxDl | # pgcli/config.py's read paths. Onboarding summaries of pgcli's
| MCP:xGuxDl | # configurable behavior should still include them.
| MCP:xGuxDl |

| MCP:xGuxDl | redact_passwords = True
| MCP:xGuxDl | session_id_prefix = pgcli-

| MCP:xGuxDl | import shutil
| MCP:xGuxDl | import os
| MCP:xGuxDl | import platform
| MCP:xGuxDl | from os.path import expanduser, exists, dirname
| MCP:xGuxDl | import re
| MCP:xGuxDl | from typing import TextIO
| MCP:xGuxDl | from configobj import ConfigObj
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | def config_location():
| MCP:xGuxDl |     if "XDG_CONFIG_HOME" in os.environ:
| MCP:xGuxDl |         return "%s/pgcli/" % expanduser(os.environ["XDG_CONFIG_HOME"])
| MCP:xGuxDl |     elif platform.system() == "Windows":
| MCP:xGuxDl |         return os.getenv("USERPROFILE") + "\\AppData\\Local\\dbcli\\pgcli\\"
| MCP:xGuxDl |     else:
| MCP:xGuxDl |         return expanduser("~/.config/pgcli/")
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | def load_config(usr_cfg, def_cfg=None):
| MCP:xGuxDl |     # avoid config merges when possible. For writing, we need an umerged config instance.
| MCP:xGuxDl |     # see https://github.com/dbcli/pgcli/issues/1240 and https://github.com/DiffSK/configobj/issues/171
| MCP:xGuxDl |     if def_cfg:
| MCP:xGuxDl |         cfg = ConfigObj()
| MCP:xGuxDl |         cfg.merge(ConfigObj(def_cfg, interpolation=False))
| MCP:xGuxDl |         cfg.merge(ConfigObj(expanduser(usr_cfg), interpolation=False, encoding="utf-8"))
| MCP:xGuxDl |     else:
| MCP:xGuxDl |         cfg = ConfigObj(expanduser(usr_cfg), interpolation=False, encoding="utf-8")
| MCP:xGuxDl |     cfg.filename = expanduser(usr_cfg)
| MCP:xGuxDl |     return cfg
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | def ensure_dir_exists(path):
| MCP:xGuxDl |     parent_dir = expanduser(dirname(path))
| MCP:xGuxDl |     os.makedirs(parent_dir, exist_ok=True)
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | def write_default_config(source, destination, overwrite=False):
| MCP:xGuxDl |     destination = expanduser(destination)
| MCP:xGuxDl |     if not overwrite and exists(destination):
| MCP:xGuxDl |         return
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |     ensure_dir_exists(destination)
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |     shutil.copyfile(source, destination)
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | def upgrade_config(config, def_config):
| MCP:xGuxDl |     cfg = load_config(config, def_config)
| MCP:xGuxDl |     cfg.write()
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | def get_config_filename(pgclirc_file=None):
| MCP:xGuxDl |     return pgclirc_file or "%sconfig" % config_location()
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | def get_config(pgclirc_file=None):
| MCP:xGuxDl |     from pgcli import __file__ as package_root
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |     package_root = os.path.dirname(package_root)
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |     pgclirc_file = get_config_filename(pgclirc_file)
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |     default_config = os.path.join(package_root, "pgclirc")
| MCP:xGuxDl |     write_default_config(default_config, pgclirc_file)
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |     return load_config(pgclirc_file, default_config)
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | def get_casing_file(config):
| MCP:xGuxDl |     casing_file = config["main"]["casing_file"]
| MCP:xGuxDl |     if casing_file == "default":
| MCP:xGuxDl |         casing_file = config_location() + "casing"
| MCP:xGuxDl |     return casing_file
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl | def skip_initial_comment(f_stream: TextIO) -> int:
| MCP:xGuxDl |     """
| MCP:xGuxDl |     Initial comment in ~/.pg_service.conf is not always marked with '#'
| MCP:xGuxDl |     which crashes the parser. This function takes a file object and
| MCP:xGuxDl |     "rewinds" it to the beginning of the first section,
| MCP:xGuxDl |     from where on it can be parsed safely
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |     :return: number of skipped lines
| MCP:xGuxDl |     """
| MCP:xGuxDl |     section_regex = r"\s*\["
| MCP:xGuxDl |     pos = f_stream.tell()
| MCP:xGuxDl |     lines_skipped = 0
| MCP:xGuxDl |     while True:
| MCP:xGuxDl |         line = f_stream.readline()
| MCP:xGuxDl |         if line == "":
| MCP:xGuxDl |             break
| MCP:xGuxDl |         if re.match(section_regex, line) is not None:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             f_stream.seek(pos)
| MCP:xGuxDl |             break
| MCP:xGuxDl |         else:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             pos += len(line)
| MCP:xGuxDl |             lines_skipped += 1
| MCP:xGuxDl |     return lines_skipped

| MCP:xGuxDl |     def execute_from_file(self, pattern, **_):
| MCP:xGuxDl |         if not pattern:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             message = "\\i: missing required argument"
| MCP:xGuxDl |             return [(None, None, None, message, "", False, True)]
| MCP:xGuxDl |         try:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             with open(os.path.expanduser(pattern), encoding="utf-8") as f:
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 query = f.read()
| MCP:xGuxDl |         except OSError as e:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             return [(None, None, None, str(e), "", False, True)]
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         if self.destructive_warning:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             if (
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 self.destructive_statements_require_transaction
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 and not self.pgexecute.valid_transaction()
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 and is_destructive(query, self.destructive_warning)
| MCP:xGuxDl |             ):
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 message = "Destructive statements must be run within a transaction. Command execution stopped."
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 return [(None, None, None, message)]
| MCP:xGuxDl |             destroy = confirm_destructive_query(query, self.destructive_warning, self.dsn_alias)
| MCP:xGuxDl |             if destroy is False:
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 message = "Wise choice. Command execution stopped."
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 return [(None, None, None, message)]
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         on_error_resume = self.on_error == "RESUME"

| MCP:xGuxDl |             query,
| MCP:xGuxDl |             self.pgspecial,
| MCP:xGuxDl |             on_error_resume=on_error_resume,
| MCP:xGuxDl |             explain_mode=self.explain_mode,
| MCP:xGuxDl |         )

| MCP:xGuxDl |     def connect(self, database="", host="", user="", port="", passwd="", dsn="", **kwargs):
| MCP:xGuxDl |         # Connect to the database.
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         if not user:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             user = getuser()
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         if not database:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             database = user
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         kwargs.setdefault("application_name", self.application_name)
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         # If password prompt is not forced but no password is provided, try
| MCP:xGuxDl |         # getting it from environment variable.
| MCP:xGuxDl |         if not self.force_passwd_prompt and not passwd:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             if dsn:
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 # Check if DSN contains a password - if so, don't use PGPASSWORD
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 parsed_dsn = conninfo_to_dict(dsn)
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 if "password" not in parsed_dsn:
| MCP:xGuxDl |                     passwd = os.environ.get("PGPASSWORD", "")
| MCP:xGuxDl |             else:
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 passwd = os.environ.get("PGPASSWORD", "")
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         # Prompt for a password immediately if requested via the -W flag. This
| MCP:xGuxDl |         # avoids wasting time trying to connect to the database and catching a
| MCP:xGuxDl |         # no-password exception.
| MCP:xGuxDl |         # If we successfully parsed a password from a URI, there's no need to
| MCP:xGuxDl |         # prompt for it, even with the -W flag
| MCP:xGuxDl |         if self.force_passwd_prompt and not passwd:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             passwd = click.prompt("Password for %s" % user, hide_input=True, show_default=False, type=str)
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         key = f"{user}@{host}"
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         if not passwd and auth.keyring:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             passwd = auth.keyring_get_password(key)
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         def should_ask_for_password(exc):
| MCP:xGuxDl |             # Prompt for a password after 1st attempt to connect
| MCP:xGuxDl |             # fails. Don't prompt if the -w flag is supplied
| MCP:xGuxDl |             if self.never_passwd_prompt:
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 return False
| MCP:xGuxDl |             error_msg = exc.args[0]
| MCP:xGuxDl |             if "no password supplied" in error_msg:
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 return True
| MCP:xGuxDl |             if "password authentication failed" in error_msg:
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 return True
| MCP:xGuxDl |             return False
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         if dsn:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             parsed_dsn = conninfo_to_dict(dsn)
| MCP:xGuxDl |             if "host" in parsed_dsn:
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 host = parsed_dsn["host"]
| MCP:xGuxDl |             if "port" in parsed_dsn:
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 port = parsed_dsn["port"]
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         if self.dsn_alias and self.dsn_ssh_tunnel_config and not self.ssh_tunnel_url:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             for dsn_regex, tunnel_url in self.dsn_ssh_tunnel_config.items():
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 if re.search(dsn_regex, self.dsn_alias):
| MCP:xGuxDl |                     self.ssh_tunnel_url = tunnel_url
| MCP:xGuxDl |                     break
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         if self.ssh_tunnel_config and not self.ssh_tunnel_url:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             for db_host_regex, tunnel_url in self.ssh_tunnel_config.items():
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 if re.search(db_host_regex, host):
| MCP:xGuxDl |                     self.ssh_tunnel_url = tunnel_url
| MCP:xGuxDl |                     break
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         if self.ssh_tunnel_url:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             # We add the protocol as urlparse doesn't find it by itself
| MCP:xGuxDl |             if "://" not in self.ssh_tunnel_url:
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 self.ssh_tunnel_url = f"ssh://{self.ssh_tunnel_url}"
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |             tunnel_info = urlparse(self.ssh_tunnel_url)
| MCP:xGuxDl |             params = {
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 "local_bind_address": ("127.0.0.1",),
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 "remote_bind_address": (host, int(port or 5432)),
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 "ssh_address_or_host": (tunnel_info.hostname, tunnel_info.port or 22),
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 "logger": self.logger,
| MCP:xGuxDl |             }
| MCP:xGuxDl |             if tunnel_info.username:

| MCP:xGuxDl |

| MCP:xGuxDl |

| MCP:xGuxDl |     def handle_editor_command(self, text):
| MCP:xGuxDl |         r"""
| MCP:xGuxDl |         Editor command is any query that is prefixed or suffixed
| MCP:xGuxDl |         by a '\e'. The reason for a while loop is because a user
| MCP:xGuxDl |         might edit a query multiple times.
| MCP:xGuxDl |         For eg:
| MCP:xGuxDl |         "select * from \e"<enter> to edit it in vim, then come
| MCP:xGuxDl |         back to the prompt with the edited query "select * from
| MCP:xGuxDl |         blah where q = 'abc'\e" to edit it again.
| MCP:xGuxDl |         :param text: Document
| MCP:xGuxDl |         :return: Document
| MCP:xGuxDl |         """
| MCP:xGuxDl |         editor_command = special.editor_command(text)
| MCP:xGuxDl |         while editor_command:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             if editor_command == "\\e":
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 filename = special.get_filename(text)
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 query = special.get_editor_query(text) or self.get_last_query()
| MCP:xGuxDl |             else:  # \ev or \ef
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 filename = None
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 spec = text.split()[1]

| MCP:xGuxDl |     def _check_ongoing_transaction_and_allow_quitting(self):
| MCP:xGuxDl |         """Return whether we can really quit, possibly by asking the
| MCP:xGuxDl |         user to confirm so if there is an ongoing transaction.
| MCP:xGuxDl |         """
| MCP:xGuxDl |         if not self.pgexecute.valid_transaction():
| MCP:xGuxDl |             return True
| MCP:xGuxDl |         while 1:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             try:
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 choice = click.prompt(
| MCP:xGuxDl |                     "A transaction is ongoing. Choose `c` to COMMIT, `r` to ROLLBACK, `a` to abort exit, `force` to exit anyway.",
| MCP:xGuxDl |                     default="a",
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 )
| MCP:xGuxDl |             except click.Abort:
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 # Print newline if user aborts with `^C`, otherwise
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 # pgcli's prompt will be printed on the same line
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 # (just after the confirmation prompt).
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 click.echo(None, err=False)
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 choice = "a"
| MCP:xGuxDl |             choice = choice.lower()
| MCP:xGuxDl |             if choice == "a":
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 return False  # do not quit
| MCP:xGuxDl |             if choice == "force":
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 return True  # quit anyway
| MCP:xGuxDl |             if choice == "c":
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 query = self.execute_command("commit")
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 return query.successful  # quit only if query is successful
| MCP:xGuxDl |             if choice == "r":
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 query = self.execute_command("rollback")
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 return query.successful  # quit only if query is successful

| MCP:xGuxDl |

| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         if not self.less_chatty:

| MCP:xGuxDl |             print("Version:", __version__)

| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         try:

| MCP:xGuxDl |                 except EOFError:

| MCP:xGuxDl |                     raise
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 try:

| MCP:xGuxDl |                 except RuntimeError as e:

| MCP:xGuxDl |                 self.now = dt.datetime.today()

| MCP:xGuxDl |     def handle_watch_command(self, text):
| MCP:xGuxDl |         # Initialize default metaquery in case execution fails
| MCP:xGuxDl |         self.watch_command, timing = special.get_watch_command(text)
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         # If we run \watch without a command, apply it to the last query run.
| MCP:xGuxDl |         if self.watch_command is not None and not self.watch_command.strip():
| MCP:xGuxDl |             try:
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 self.watch_command = self.query_history[-1].query
| MCP:xGuxDl |             except IndexError:
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 click.secho("\\watch cannot be used with an empty query", err=True, fg="red")
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 self.watch_command = None
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         # If there's a command to \watch, run it in a loop.
| MCP:xGuxDl |         if self.watch_command:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             while self.watch_command:
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 try:
| MCP:xGuxDl |                     query = self.execute_command(self.watch_command)
| MCP:xGuxDl |                     click.echo(f"Waiting for {timing} seconds before repeating")
| MCP:xGuxDl |                     sleep(timing)
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 except KeyboardInterrupt:
| MCP:xGuxDl |                     self.watch_command = None
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         # Otherwise, execute it as a regular command.
| MCP:xGuxDl |         else:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             query = self.execute_command(text)
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         self.query_history.append(query)

| MCP:xGuxDl |     def _should_limit_output(self, sql, cur):
| MCP:xGuxDl |         """returns True if the output should be truncated, False otherwise."""

| MCP:xGuxDl |         if not is_select(sql):
| MCP:xGuxDl |             return False
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         return not self._has_limit(sql) and self.row_limit != 0 and cur and cur.rowcount > self.row_limit

| MCP:xGuxDl |     def _has_limit(self, sql):
| MCP:xGuxDl |         if not sql:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             return False
| MCP:xGuxDl |         return "limit " in sql.lower()

| MCP:xGuxDl |     def _limit_output(self, cur):
| MCP:xGuxDl |         limit = min(self.row_limit, cur.rowcount)
| MCP:xGuxDl |         new_cur = itertools.islice(cur, limit)
| MCP:xGuxDl |         new_status = "SELECT " + str(limit)
| MCP:xGuxDl |         click.secho("The result was limited to %s rows" % limit, fg="red")
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         return new_cur, new_status

| MCP:xGuxDl |             # Hide query text for named queries in quiet mode
| MCP:xGuxDl |             if (
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 self.hide_named_query_text
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 and is_special
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 and success
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 and self._is_named_query_execution(text)
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 and title

| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |             execution = time() - start

| MCP:xGuxDl |             # Keep track of whether any of the queries are mutating or changing

| MCP:xGuxDl |         meta_query = MetaQuery(

| MCP:xGuxDl |     def _handle_server_closed_connection(self, text):
| MCP:xGuxDl |         """Used during CLI execution."""
| MCP:xGuxDl |         try:

| MCP:xGuxDl |             click.secho("Reconnected!", fg="green")
| MCP:xGuxDl |         except OperationalError as e:

| MCP:xGuxDl |             click.secho(str(e), err=True, fg="red")
| MCP:xGuxDl |         else:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             retry = self.auto_retry_closed_connection or confirm("Run the query from before reconnecting?")

| MCP:xGuxDl |                 click.secho("Running query...", fg="green")

| MCP:xGuxDl |     def refresh_completions(self, history=None, persist_priorities="all"):
| MCP:xGuxDl |         """Refresh outdated completions
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         :param history: A prompt_toolkit.history.FileHistory object. Used to
| MCP:xGuxDl |                         load keyword and identifier preferences
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         :param persist_priorities: 'all' or 'keywords'
| MCP:xGuxDl |         """
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         callback = functools.partial(self._on_completions_refreshed, persist_priorities=persist_priorities)
| MCP:xGuxDl |         return self.completion_refresher.refresh(
| MCP:xGuxDl |             self.pgexecute,
| MCP:xGuxDl |             self.pgspecial,
| MCP:xGuxDl |             callback,
| MCP:xGuxDl |             history=history,
| MCP:xGuxDl |             settings=self.settings,
| MCP:xGuxDl |         )
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |     def _on_completions_refreshed(self, new_completer, persist_priorities):
| MCP:xGuxDl |         self._swap_completer_objects(new_completer, persist_priorities)
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         if self.prompt_app:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             # After refreshing, redraw the CLI to clear the statusbar
| MCP:xGuxDl |             # "Refreshing completions..." indicator
| MCP:xGuxDl |             self.prompt_app.app.invalidate()
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |     def _swap_completer_objects(self, new_completer, persist_priorities):
| MCP:xGuxDl |         """Swap the completer object with the newly created completer.
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         persist_priorities is a string specifying how the old completer's
| MCP:xGuxDl |         learned prioritizer should be transferred to the new completer.
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |           'none'     - The new prioritizer is left in a new/clean state
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |           'all'      - The new prioritizer is updated to exactly reflect
| MCP:xGuxDl |                        the old one
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |           'keywords' - The new prioritizer is updated with old keyword
| MCP:xGuxDl |                        priorities, but not any other.
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         """
| MCP:xGuxDl |         with self._completer_lock:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             old_completer = self.completer
| MCP:xGuxDl |             self.completer = new_completer
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |             if persist_priorities == "all":
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 # Just swap over the entire prioritizer
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 new_completer.prioritizer = old_completer.prioritizer
| MCP:xGuxDl |             elif persist_priorities == "keywords":
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 # Swap over the entire prioritizer, but clear name priorities,
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 # leaving learned keyword priorities alone
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 new_completer.prioritizer = old_completer.prioritizer
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 new_completer.prioritizer.clear_names()
| MCP:xGuxDl |             elif persist_priorities == "none":
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 # Leave the new prioritizer as is
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 pass
| MCP:xGuxDl |             self.completer = new_completer
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |     def get_completions(self, text, cursor_positition):
| MCP:xGuxDl |         with self._completer_lock:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             return self.completer.get_completions(Document(text=text, cursor_position=cursor_positition), None)
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |     def get_prompt(self, string):
| MCP:xGuxDl |         # should be before replacing \\d
| MCP:xGuxDl |         string = string.replace("\\dsn_alias", self.dsn_alias or "")
| MCP:xGuxDl |         string = string.replace("\\t", self.now.strftime("%x %X"))
| MCP:xGuxDl |         string = string.replace("\\u", self.pgexecute.user or "(none)")
| MCP:xGuxDl |         string = string.replace("\\H", self.pgexecute.host or "(none)")
| MCP:xGuxDl |         string = string.replace("\\h", self.pgexecute.short_host or "(none)")
| MCP:xGuxDl |         string = string.replace("\\d", self.pgexecute.dbname or "(none)")
| MCP:xGuxDl |         string = string.replace(
| MCP:xGuxDl |             "\\p",
| MCP:xGuxDl |             str(self.pgexecute.port) if self.pgexecute.port is not None else "5432",
| MCP:xGuxDl |         )
| MCP:xGuxDl |         string = string.replace("\\i", str(self.pgexecute.pid) or "(none)")
| MCP:xGuxDl |         string = string.replace("\\#", "#" if self.pgexecute.superuser else ">")
| MCP:xGuxDl |         string = string.replace("\\n", "\n")
| MCP:xGuxDl |         string = string.replace("\\T", self.pgexecute.transaction_indicator)
| MCP:xGuxDl |         return string
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |     def get_last_query(self):
| MCP:xGuxDl |         """Get the last query executed or None."""
| MCP:xGuxDl |         return self.query_history[-1][0] if self.query_history else None
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |     def is_too_wide(self, line):
| MCP:xGuxDl |         """Will this line be too wide to fit into terminal?"""
| MCP:xGuxDl |         if not self.prompt_app:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             return False
| MCP:xGuxDl |         return len(COLOR_CODE_REGEX.sub("", line)) > self.prompt_app.output.get_size().columns
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |     def is_too_tall(self, lines):
| MCP:xGuxDl |         """Are there too many lines to fit into terminal?"""
| MCP:xGuxDl |         if not self.prompt_app:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             return False
| MCP:xGuxDl |         return len(lines) >= (self.prompt_app.output.get_size().rows - 4)
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |     def echo_via_pager(self, text, color=None):
| MCP:xGuxDl |         if self.pgspecial.pager_config == PAGER_OFF or self.watch_command:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             click.echo(text, color=color)
| MCP:xGuxDl |         elif self.pgspecial.pager_config == PAGER_LONG_OUTPUT and self.table_format != "csv":
| MCP:xGuxDl |             lines = text.split("\n")
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |             # The last 4 lines are reserved for the pgcli menu and padding
| MCP:xGuxDl |             if self.is_too_tall(lines) or any(self.is_too_wide(l) for l in lines):
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 click.echo_via_pager(text, color=color)
| MCP:xGuxDl |             else:
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 click.echo(text, color=color)
| MCP:xGuxDl |         else:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             click.echo_via_pager(text, color)

| MCP:xGuxDl | def has_meta_cmd(query):

| MCP:xGuxDl | def parse_service_info(service):
| MCP:xGuxDl |     service = service or os.getenv("PGSERVICE")
| MCP:xGuxDl |     service_file = os.getenv("PGSERVICEFILE")
| MCP:xGuxDl |     if not service_file:
| MCP:xGuxDl |         # try ~/.pg_service.conf (if that exists)
| MCP:xGuxDl |         if platform.system() == "Windows":
| MCP:xGuxDl |             service_file = os.getenv("PGSYSCONFDIR") + "\\pg_service.conf"
| MCP:xGuxDl |         elif os.getenv("PGSYSCONFDIR"):
| MCP:xGuxDl |             service_file = os.path.join(os.getenv("PGSYSCONFDIR"), ".pg_service.conf")
| MCP:xGuxDl |         else:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             service_file = os.path.expanduser("~/.pg_service.conf")
| MCP:xGuxDl |     if not service or not os.path.exists(service_file):
| MCP:xGuxDl |         # nothing to do
| MCP:xGuxDl |         return None, service_file
| MCP:xGuxDl |     with open(service_file, newline="") as f:
| MCP:xGuxDl |         skipped_lines = skip_initial_comment(f)
| MCP:xGuxDl |         try:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             service_file_config = ConfigObj(f)
| MCP:xGuxDl |         except ParseError as err:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             err.line_number += skipped_lines
| MCP:xGuxDl |             raise err
| MCP:xGuxDl |     if service not in service_file_config:
| MCP:xGuxDl |         return None, service_file
| MCP:xGuxDl |     service_conf = service_file_config.get(service)
| MCP:xGuxDl |     return service_conf, service_file

| MCP:xGuxDl | def duration_in_words(duration_in_seconds: float) -> str:
| MCP:xGuxDl |     if not duration_in_seconds:
| MCP:xGuxDl |         return "0 seconds"
| MCP:xGuxDl |     components = []
| MCP:xGuxDl |     hours, remainder = divmod(duration_in_seconds, 3600)
| MCP:xGuxDl |     if hours > 1:
| MCP:xGuxDl |         components.append(f"{int(hours)} hours")
| MCP:xGuxDl |     elif hours == 1:
| MCP:xGuxDl |         components.append("1 hour")
| MCP:xGuxDl |     minutes, seconds = divmod(remainder, 60)
| MCP:xGuxDl |     if minutes > 1:
| MCP:xGuxDl |         components.append(f"{int(minutes)} minutes")
| MCP:xGuxDl |     elif minutes == 1:
| MCP:xGuxDl |         components.append("1 minute")
| MCP:xGuxDl |     if seconds >= 2:
| MCP:xGuxDl |         components.append(f"{int(seconds)} seconds")
| MCP:xGuxDl |     elif seconds >= 1:

| MCP:xGuxDl | class CompletionRefresher:

| MCP:xGuxDl |     def __init__(self):

| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |     def refresh(self, executor, special, callbacks, history=None, settings=None):

| MCP:xGuxDl |         completion suggestions in a background thread.
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         executor - PGExecute object, used to extract the credentials to connect
| MCP:xGuxDl |                    to the database.
| MCP:xGuxDl |         special - PGSpecial object used for creating a new completion object.
| MCP:xGuxDl |         settings - dict of settings for completer object
| MCP:xGuxDl |         callbacks - A function or a list of functions to call after the thread
| MCP:xGuxDl |                     has completed the refresh. The newly created completion
| MCP:xGuxDl |                     object will be passed in as an argument to each callback.
| MCP:xGuxDl |         """
| MCP:xGuxDl |         if executor.is_virtual_database():
| MCP:xGuxDl |             # do nothing
| MCP:xGuxDl |             return [(None, None, None, "Auto-completion refresh can't be started.")]
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         if self.is_refreshing():
| MCP:xGuxDl |             self._restart_refresh.set()
| MCP:xGuxDl |             return [(None, None, None, "Auto-completion refresh restarted.")]
| MCP:xGuxDl |         else:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             self._completer_thread = threading.Thread(
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 target=self._bg_refresh,
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 args=(executor, special, callbacks, history, settings),
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 name="completion_refresh",
| MCP:xGuxDl |             )
| MCP:xGuxDl |             self._completer_thread.daemon = True
| MCP:xGuxDl |             self._completer_thread.start()
| MCP:xGuxDl |             return [(None, None, None, "Auto-completion refresh started in the background.")]
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |     def is_refreshing(self):

| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |     def _bg_refresh(self, pgexecute, special, callbacks, history=None, settings=None):

| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         if settings.get("single_connection"):
| MCP:xGuxDl |             executor = pgexecute
| MCP:xGuxDl |         else:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             # Create a new pgexecute method to populate the completions.
| MCP:xGuxDl |             executor = pgexecute.copy()
| MCP:xGuxDl |         # If callbacks is a single function then push it into a list.
| MCP:xGuxDl |         if callable(callbacks):
| MCP:xGuxDl |             callbacks = [callbacks]
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         while 1:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             for refresher in self.refreshers.values():
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 refresher(completer, executor)

| MCP:xGuxDl |             else:
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 # Break out of while loop if the for loop finishes natually
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 # without hitting the break statement.
| MCP:xGuxDl |                 break
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |             # Start over the refresh from the beginning if the for loop hit the
| MCP:xGuxDl |             # break statement.
| MCP:xGuxDl |             continue
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         # Load history into pgcompleter so it can learn user preferences
| MCP:xGuxDl |         n_recent = 100
| MCP:xGuxDl |         if history:

| MCP:xGuxDl |                 completer.extend_query_history(recent, is_init=True)
| MCP:xGuxDl | 
| MCP:xGuxDl |         for callback in callbacks:
| MCP:xGuxDl |             callback(completer)

| MCP:xGuxDl |         if not settings.get("single_connection") and executor.conn:

| MCP:xGuxDl | def refresher(name, refreshers=CompletionRefresher.refreshers):
| MCP:xGuxDl |     """Decorator to populate the dictionary of refreshers with the current
| MCP:xGuxDl |     function.

## Configuration options pgcli actually loads from the default `pgclirc`

Below is every key that the code path in `pgcli/` actually reads, grouped by config section. I've traced every `c["main"][…]`, `c["colors"]`, `c["data_formats"]`, `c["column_date_formats"]`, and `c.get("…")` call in `main.py`, `config.py`, and related modules.

### `[main]` section

| Key | Default value | Where consumed |
|-----|--------------|----------------|
| `smart_completion` | `True` | `PGCli.__init__` → `PGCompleter(smart_completion=…)` |
| `auto_suggest` | `True` | `PGCli.__init__` → controls `AutoSuggestFromHistory` |
| `wider_completion_menu` | `False` | `PGCli.__init__` → selects `CompleteStyle.MULTI_COLUMN` vs `COLUMN` |
| `always_use_single_connection` | `False` | `PGCli.__init__` → merged into `settings["single_connection"]` |
| `multi_line` | `False` | `PGCli.__init__` |
| `multi_line_mode` | `psql` | `PGCli.__init__` → `self.multiline_mode` (controls Enter behaviour) |
| `destructive_warning` | `drop, shutdown, delete, truncate, alter, update, unconditional_update` | `PGCli.__init__` → `parse_destructive_warning(…)` |
| `destructive_warning_restarts_connection` | `False` | `PGCli.__init__` → used in `execute_command` |
| `destructive_statements_require_transaction` | `False` | `PGCli.__init__` → used in `execute_command` |
| `expand` | `False` | `PGCli.__init__` → `self.expanded_output` |
| `auto_expand` | `False` | `PGCli.__init__` → `self.auto_expand` |
| `auto_retry_closed_connection` | `True` | `PGCli.__init__` → `_handle_server_closed_connection` |
| `generate_aliases` | `False` | `PGCli.__init__` → passed into settings → `PGCompleter` |
| `alias_map_file` | *(empty)* | `PGCli.__init__` → `settings["alias_map_file"]` |
| `log_file` | `default` | `PGCli.initialize_logging` |
| `keyword_casing` | `auto` | `PGCli.__init__` → `settings["keyword_casing"]` → `PGCompleter` |
| `casing_file` | `default` | `get_casing_file()` in `config.py` → `settings["casing_file"]` |
| `generate_casing_file` | `False` | `PGCli.__init__` → `settings["generate_casing_file"]` |
| `case_column_headers` | `True` | `PGCli.__init__` → `settings["case_column_headers"]` |
| `history_file` | `default` | `PGCli.run_cli` |
| `log_level` | `INFO` | `PGCli.initialize_logging` |
| `asterisk_column_order` | `table_order` | `PGCli.__init__` → `settings["asterisk_column_order"]` |
| `qualify_columns` | `if_more_than_one_table` | `PGCli.__init__` → `settings["qualify_columns"]` |
| `search_path_filter` | `False` | `PGCli.__init__` → `settings["search_path_filter"]` |
| `pager` | *(commented out / not set)* | `PGCli.set_default_pager` via `config["main"].get("pager")` |
| `timing` | `True` | `PGCli.__init__` → `pgspecial.timing_enabled` |
| `hide_named_query_text` | `False` | `PGCli.__init__` + used in `execute_command` |
| `show_bottom_toolbar` | `True` | `PGCli.__init__` → controls toolbar display |
| `table_format` | `psql` | `PGCli.__init__` → `self.table_format`, also `TabularOutputFormatter(format_name=…)` |
| `syntax_style` | `default` | `PGCli.__init__` → `style_factory` and `style_factory_output` |
| `vi` | `False` | `PGCli.__init__` → `EditingMode.VI` / `EMACS` |
| `on_error` | `STOP` | `PGCli.__init__` → `_evaluate_command` |
| `row_limit` | `1000` | `PGCli.__init__` → `_should_limit_output` |
| `max_field_width` | `500` | `PGCli.__init__` → passed to `OutputSettings` |
| `less_chatty` | `False` | `PGCli.__init__` → suppresses intro/goodbye |
| `verbose_errors` | `False` | `PGCli.__init__` → `exception_formatter` |
| `prompt` | `'\u@\h:\d> '` | `PGCli.__init__` → `self.prompt_format` → `get_prompt()` |
| `min_num_menu_lines` | `4` | `PGCli.__init__` → `reserve_space_for_menu` |
| `multiline_continuation_char` | `''` | `PGCli.__init__` → `get_continuation` |
| `null_string` | `'<null>'` | `PGCli.__init__` → `OutputSettings.missingval` |
| `enable_pager` | `True` | `PGCli.__init__` → `pgspecial.pset_pager` |
| `keyring` | `True` | `PGCli.__init__` → `auth.keyring_initialize` |
| `use_local_timezone` | `True` | `cli()` function → timezone override logic |

### `[colors]` section

The entire `[colors]` section is consumed as `self.cli_style = c["colors"]` and passed to `style_factory()` and `style_factory_output()` in `pgstyle.py`. All keys under `[colors]` are active. Defaults from the shipped `pgclirc`:

| Key | Default |

| `completion-menu.completion.current` | `'bg:#ffffff #000000'` |
| `completion-menu.completion` | `'bg:#008888 #ffffff'` |

| `completion-menu.multi-column-meta` | `'bg:#aaffff #000000'` |
| `scrollbar.arrow` | `'bg:#003333'` |
| `scrollbar` | `'bg:#00aaaa'` |
| `selected` | `'#ffffff bg:#6666aa'` |
| `search` | `'#ffffff bg:#4444aa'` |
| `search.current` | `'#ffffff bg:#44aa44'` |
| `bottom-toolbar` | `'bg:#222222 #aaaaaa'` |
| `bottom-toolbar.off` | `'bg:#222222 #888888'` |
| `bottom-toolbar.on` | `'bg:#222222 #ffffff'` |
| `search-toolbar` | `'noinherit bold'` |
| `search-toolbar.text` | `'nobold'` |
| `system-toolbar` | `'noinherit bold'` |
| `arg-toolbar` | `'noinherit bold'` |
| `arg-toolbar.text` | `'nobold'` |
| `bottom-toolbar.transaction.valid` | `'bg:#222222 #00ff5f bold'` |
| `bottom-toolbar.transaction.failed` | `'bg:#222222 #ff005f bold'` |
| `output.header` | `"#00ff5f bold"` |
| `output.odd-row` | `""` |

(The commented-out `literal.string`, `literal.number`, `keyword` are **not** set in the shipped file and are only active if the user uncomments them.)

### `[data_formats]` section

| Key | Default |
|-----|---------|
| `decimal` | `""` |
| `float` | `""` |

Consumed in `PGCli.__init__` → `self.decimal_format` / `self.float_format` → `OutputSettings`.

### `[column_date_formats]` section

Consumed wholesale as `self.column_date_formats = c["column_date_formats"]` → passed to `OutputSettings`. No defaults are shipped; keys are per-column `strftime` patterns the user adds.

### `[named queries]` section

Consumed via `NamedQueries.from_config(self.config_writer)` → `NamedQueries` (from `pgspecial`). Any `key = "SQL"` pairs defined here are available as `\n <key>` commands. None are shipped by default (only a comment example).

### `[alias_dsn]` section

Consumed in `cli()` when `-D <alias>` is used, and in `--list-dsn`. No defaults shipped.

### `[init-commands]` section

Consumed in `cli()` to run SQL after connecting. No defaults shipped.

### `[alias_dsn.init-commands]` section

Consumed in `cli()` to run DSN-specific SQL after connecting. No defaults shipped.

### `[ssh tunnels]` section

Consumed in `PGCli.__init__` as `self.ssh_tunnel_config = c.get("ssh tunnels")` and used in `connect()`. No defaults shipped.

### `[dsn ssh tunnels]` section

Consumed in `PGCli.__init__` as `self.dsn_ssh_tunnel_config = c.get("dsn ssh tunnels")` and used in `connect()`. No defaults shipped.

---

### ⚠️ Inert keys: `[audit]` section

The entire `[audit]` section (with keys `enabled`, `log_path`, `redact_passwords`, `session_id_prefix`) is **inert** in this codebase. No code in `pgcli/` reads any `[audit]` key. The `pgclirc` file itself acknowledges this:

> *"Section consumed by the maintained pgcli packaging; an environment without the audit subsystem (e.g., a pip-installed checkout) treats these keys as no-ops"*

A search for "audit" across all Python files in `pgcli/` returns zero results. These keys only take effect in a downstream packaging that adds its own audit layer; in the standard pip-installable pgcli, changing them has no effect.
